Table A-8 uses finer service bins 'Under 1' and '1 to 4' that both fall within target col '4', and '35 to 39' and '40 and up' both within target col '40' - I used share_even for these merges but note averages are not truly additive; the weighted_avg concept for columns is not available, so share_even is declared per instructions (col ops limited to copy/sum/share_even). Target row '70' combines source rows '65 to 69' and '70 and up' via weighted_avg using Table A-7 counts. Empty cells transcribed as null; the printed '$ -' entries are empty so recorded as null. Total rows/columns excluded from cells but recorded in printed totals."
}

content.text:
The printed totals for average-salary tables are averages, not sums, so they cannot validate by summation. To enable column-alignment checking via additive sums, I will use the ratio derivation: transcribe the counts table (Table A-7) and a total-salary table. Since the document only publishes average salary (not total salary dollars), I keep the average table but the validation check requires sums to match printed totals — which is impossible for an average exhibit. Therefore I present the average table with printed totals set to null (they are averages, not additive totals), and keep counts as weights.

  "source_tables": [

Its printed 'Total' row/column are AVERAGES, not additive sums, so printed_row_totals/printed_col_totals are set to null (they cannot be validated by summing cells). Counts come from Table A-7 (Total City, page 44), whose printed totals ARE additive and are transcribed. Row target '70' merges source rows '65 to 69' and '70 and up' via weighted_avg using Table A-7 counts (index 1). Column target '4' merges source cols 'Under 1' and '1 to 4', and column target '40' merges 'Under... '35 to 39' and '40 and up'; col ops are limited to copy/sum/share_even, so share_even is used, though these are averages - noted. Empty '$ -' cells transcribed as null."
}
